entries.failure_mode: Incorrect P0 parity bit
entries.effect: Slave node rejects header or interprets wrong frame
entries.mitigation: Unit tests with golden vector (id=0x10 → pid=0xD0)

entries.failure_mode: Incorrect P1 parity bit
entries.effect: Slave node rejects header or interprets wrong frame
entries.mitigation: Unit tests with golden vector (id=0x10 → pid=0xD0)

entries.failure_mode: Carry-around not applied (subtract 0x100 instead of 0xFF)
entries.effect: Wrong checksum byte; slave node detects checksum error and discards frame
entries.mitigation: Carry-around unit test with multi-byte data that overflows

entries.failure_mode: Checksum not inverted
entries.effect: Wrong checksum byte; slave node detects checksum error
entries.mitigation: Unit tests comparing against known-good checksum values

entries.failure_mode: Enhanced checksum accepted for diagnostic frame ID 0x3C
entries.effect: Incorrect diagnostic processing; potential slave node misconfiguration
entries.mitigation: validate_frame checks id ∈ {0x3C, 0x3D} and rejects Enhanced

entries.failure_mode: Frame ID > 0x3F not rejected
entries.effect: Bus protocol violation; undefined slave node behaviour
entries.mitigation: validate_frame checks id ≤ LIN_MAX_ID (0x3F)

entries.failure_mode: Data length > 8 not rejected
entries.effect: Bus protocol violation; undefined slave node behaviour
entries.mitigation: validate_frame checks data.len() ≤ LIN_MAX_DATA_LEN (8)

entries.failure_mode: NoResponse not returned when slave response absent
entries.effect: Caller assumes success; stale data used by application
entries.mitigation: Unit and integration tests verify NoResponse on missing entry

entries.failure_mode: NoResponse does not map to relay::Error::Timeout
entries.effect: RELAY runtime receives wrong error sentinel; incorrect timeout handling
entries.mitigation: Integration test asserts NoResponse.kind() == relay::Error::Timeout

entries.failure_mode: Back-pressure blocks send_header indefinitely
entries.effect: Bus deadlock; no further frames delivered
entries.mitigation: DropNewest/DropOldest policy; push() is non-blocking

entries.failure_mode: CRC polynomial wrong (0x1021 not used)
entries.effect: CRC check passes for corrupt data; H-07 hazard realised
entries.mitigation: Golden-vector test verifies CRC-16/CCITT-FALSE (poly=0x1021, init=0xFFFF)

entries.failure_mode: CRC slot not zeroed before computing CRC over header
entries.effect: CRC verification always fails or always passes incorrectly
entries.mitigation: Unit test: corrupt CRC byte, verify CrcMismatch returned

entries.failure_mode: Sequence counter comparison wrong — gap not detected
entries.effect: Replayed frame accepted; stale data used by safety function
entries.mitigation: Unit test: skip seq=1, verify SequenceGap on seq=2

entries.failure_mode: SequenceCounter not atomically incremented
entries.effect: Two concurrent protect() calls produce identical counters; Receiver raises false SequenceGap
entries.mitigation: AtomicU32::fetch_add with SeqCst ordering; 8-thread concurrent test verifies distinct counters

entries.failure_mode: HeaderTooShort check absent — buffer overread
entries.effect: Undefined behaviour reading past end of data slice
entries.mitigation: Rust bounds-check prevents OOB; unit test verifies E2eError::HeaderTooShort returned for 9-byte input

entries.failure_mode: Returned payload is a view into the input slice (aliasing)
entries.effect: Caller modifying returned buffer corrupts internal receiver state
entries.mitigation: Ownership semantics: payload.to_vec() creates an owned Vec; test verifies independence

entries.failure_mode: DataID or SourceID not embedded in header
entries.effect: Receiver CRC covers different data; mismatch on valid frame
entries.mitigation: Unit test reads bytes 0–1 and 2–3 of header, verifies DataID and SourceID

entries.failure_mode: parse() panics on malformed LDF input
entries.effect: Process aborts; entire LIN stack unavailable
entries.mitigation: Unit test with empty/garbage input verifies Ok(Db) with zero entries returned

entries.failure_mode: parse_hex() silently wraps on value > 255
entries.effect: Frame ID stored as id > 0x3F; schedule validation passes but bus rejects
entries.mitigation: parse_hex uses u8 target type; value > 255 parsed as 0 (wrapping) — test exercises boundary

entries.failure_mode: Schedule entry ID from LDF not validated before passing to MasterNode
entries.effect: MasterNode::set_schedule call with id > 0x3F; bus protocol violation
entries.mitigation: Integration test iterates LDF schedule entries and asserts id ≤ LIN_MAX_ID

entries.failure_mode: set_response does not take lock before updating registered set
entries.effect: registered_ids() returns inconsistent state during concurrent call
entries.mitigation: Mutex<HashSet<u8>> guards all registered_ids mutations; Bus::publish uses its own lock

entries.failure_mode: set_response(None) does not remove ID from registered set
entries.effect: registered_ids() still lists id; application believes response is active when it is not
entries.mitigation: Unit test: set_response(id, None) then verify registered_ids() excludes id

entries.failure_mode: ID overflow 0x40 not rejected
entries.effect: Bus stores response under id & 0x3F causing aliasing collision
entries.mitigation: set_response checks id > LIN_MAX_ID and returns Error::InvalidFrame; test verifies

entries.failure_mode: on_error callback not invoked for NoResponse
entries.effect: Application unaware of missing slave; stale actuation persists
entries.mitigation: Integration test: schedule entry with no response, verify on_error invoked

entries.failure_mode: ctx.done() not checked; loop does not terminate
entries.effect: Bus continues sending after shutdown; slave nodes in unexpected state
entries.mitigation: Integration test with 50 ms timeout; run() returns within deadline

entries.failure_mode: Empty schedule accepted by run()
entries.effect: run() panics or loops without sending; watchdog timer not reset
entries.mitigation: run() returns Error::Other when schedule is empty; unit test verifies

entries.failure_mode: Non-LIN protocol message (e.g., CAN = 1) not rejected
entries.effect: CAN frame ID interpreted as LIN frame ID; wrong slave addressed
entries.mitigation: from_message must check message.protocol == Protocol::Lin (3)

entries.failure_mode: from_message returns Ok for Protocol::Can message
entries.effect: H-12: frame from different bus interpreted as LIN; wrong actuator addressed
entries.mitigation: REQ-SEC-002: from_message rejects protocol != Lin; integration test verifies

entries.failure_mode: to_message emits wrong protocol value (not 3)
entries.effect: RELAY runtime routes message to wrong adapter
entries.mitigation: Unit test asserts msg.protocol == Protocol::Lin (3)

entries.failure_mode: validate_frame not called before broadcast
entries.effect: Invalid frame (ID > 0x3F, oversized data) delivered to subscribers
entries.mitigation: send_header calls validate_frame before broadcast; integration test verifies rejection
